Package org.solenopsis.soap.metadata

Class WaveXmd

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.Metadata
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.WaveXmd
public class WaveXmd extends Metadata

Java class for WaveXmd complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="WaveXmd">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}Metadata">
       <sequence>
         <element name="application" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="dataset" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="datasetConnector" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="datasetFullyQualifiedName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="dates" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}WaveXmdDate" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="dimensions" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}WaveXmdDimension" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="measures" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}WaveXmdMeasure" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="organizations" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}WaveXmdOrganization" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="origin" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="type" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="waveVisualization" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

    • getDates

      public List<WaveXmdDate> getDates()
      Gets the value of the dates property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the dates property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getDates().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list WaveXmdDate

      Returns:
      The value of the dates property.

    • getDimensions

      public List<WaveXmdDimension> getDimensions()
      Gets the value of the dimensions property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the dimensions property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getDimensions().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list WaveXmdDimension

      Returns:
      The value of the dimensions property.

      public List<WaveXmdMeasure> getMeasures()
      Gets the value of the measures property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the measures property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getMeasures().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list WaveXmdMeasure

      Returns:
      The value of the measures property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the organizations property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getOrganizations().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list WaveXmdOrganization

      Returns:
      The value of the organizations property.
